title: Audit Trail Exception Backfill
description: A reconciliation and back-office workflow that resolves breaks, validates accounting alignment, and closes audit-critical exceptions. Focus case: audit trail exception backfill.
assumptions: Each audit_exception_case represents a single reconciled target with unique identity throughout the workflow.
Ledger accounts and mapping records referenced exist and are available for reservation when required.
Analyst and approver resources (T11 and T9) are available within expected SLA to avoid deadlock.
Audit_check definitions (T10) are authoritative and immutable for the duration of a case resolution.
Statement feeds and source files are ingested and uniquely identifiable by statement_feed (T8) and source_file (T6).
risks: Incorrect ledger_account or account_mapping selection can lead to material misstatement if backfill is posted to wrong accounts.
Failure to reserve/release ledger_entry items atomically can produce double postings or lost entries.
Insufficient or missing reference_documentation (T12) may block analyst validation, stalling closure.
Overreliance on manual approver_tokens (T9) increases operational latency and introduces approval bottlenecks.
Incomplete audit_check coverage or misconfigured audit_check rules (T10) may permit improper closure without required controls.
